title: Warm Tunisian Wood Ears Tagine
description: A buttery, molten Tunisian tagine featuring wood ear mushrooms in a fragrant, sugar-free sauce, perfect for a show‑off dinner.
instructions: Soak wood ear mushrooms in warm water for 20 minutes, then drain and slice into bite‑size pieces.
Heat olive oil in a tagine over medium heat and add butter, letting it melt.
Stir in tomato paste, cumin, coriander, and smoked paprika until the mixture becomes fragrant.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring to a gentle simmer, then add the sliced wood ears.
Cook the mixture for 20 minutes, allowing the mushrooms to soften and absorb the spices.
Add lemon juice, chopped parsley, salt, and pepper, and simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, then remove from heat.
Serve the tagine hot, spooning the molten sauce over couscous or crusty bread.
